name: Maldives
tagline: Overwater bungalows, crystal lagoons, and world-class diving
bestFor: Honeymoons, luxury escapes, and scuba diving enthusiasts
highlights: Overwater bungalows with direct lagoon access
World's best snorkeling and diving
Bioluminescent beaches on Vaadhoo Island
Private island resorts
Whale shark encounters
tips: Book seaplane transfers in advance — slots fill up fast
Travel in Nov-Apr to avoid monsoon season
All-inclusive resorts are common and often worth it
Bring USD cash as ATMs are rare on outer atolls
A travel rewards card can offset the high resort costs significantly
travelCardTip: Use a travel rewards card with no foreign transaction fees. Chase Sapphire Preferred or Capital One Venture X are ideal here — the sign-up bonus alone can cover a night.

name: Amalfi Coast
tagline: Dramatic cliffside villages, turquoise sea, and fresh limoncello
bestFor: Couples, culture lovers, and coastal road trip fans
bestSeason: May to June, September to October
highlights: Drive the iconic cliffside SS163 coastal road
Explore Positano, Ravello, and Amalfi town
Boat trips to the Blue Grotto and Li Galli islands
Fresh seafood and limoncello tastings
Hike the Path of the Gods (Sentiero degli Dei)
tips: Avoid July–August — overcrowded and scorching
Base yourself in Praiano for lower prices than Positano
Take the ferry between towns instead of driving
Book restaurants a few days ahead in peak season
Combo with Naples and Pompeii for a full southern Italy trip
cons: Roads are narrow and stressful to drive
travelCardTip: Amex Gold earns 3x on flights and 4x on dining — perfect for a food-heavy Italian trip. No foreign transaction fees essential.

name: Greek Islands
tagline: White-washed villages, sunset views, and ancient history
bestFor: Island hoppers, history buffs, and sunset chasers
highlights: Santorini caldera sunsets from Oia
Mykonos nightlife and windmills
Sailing between Cyclades islands
Ancient ruins on Rhodes and Crete
Black sand beaches of Perissa
tips: Ferry between islands — Blue Star Ferries is reliable
Book Santorini accommodation 6+ months ahead
Visit Crete for fewer crowds and more authentic Greece
Shoulder season (May, Sep) has great weather and lower prices
Rent a quad bike or ATV to explore smaller islands
flightFrom: Fly into Athens (ATH), then ferry or domestic flight
pros: Affordable compared to other Mediterranean spots
travelCardTip: A Chase Sapphire Preferred covers trip cancellation insurance — useful if ferries get cancelled due to weather. No foreign transaction fee is a must.

name: British Virgin Islands
tagline: The sailing capital of the world — steady trade winds, calm anchorages
bestFor: Sailors, divers, and bareboat charter enthusiasts
highlights: Sail the Sir Francis Drake Channel
The Baths National Park on Virgin Gorda
Bitter End Yacht Club (rebuilt post-Irma)
Snorkel the wreck of the RMS Rhone
Sundowners at Soggy Dollar Bar (Jost Van Dyke)
tips: Charter a bareboat if you're a competent sailor — it's the best way to see the BVI
Book charters 6–12 months ahead for peak season
Jost Van Dyke is a must — tiny island, legendary beach bars
Carry USD cash — some beach bars don't take cards
Check entry requirements (cruising permits needed)
flightFrom: Fly into Tortola (EIS) via San Juan or St. Thomas
travelCardTip: Amex Platinum's $200 airline credit and lounge access make the multi-stop journey much more bearable. Capital One Venture X's 2x on everything is great for charter and villa costs.

name: Bali, Indonesia
tagline: Rice terraces, surf breaks, temples, and spa culture all in one place
bestFor: Budget travelers, surfers, yogis, and digital nomads
language: Bahasa Indonesian, English in tourist areas
highlights: Sunrise at Mount Batur volcano
Surfing at Uluwatu and Canggu
Tegallalang Rice Terraces
Ubud's art and wellness scene
Temple hopping (Tanah Lot, Besakih)
tips: Rent a scooter in Ubud — it's the best way to get around
Seminyak for nightlife, Ubud for culture, Uluwatu for surf
Avoid plastic: bring a reusable water bottle
Dry season (April–Oct) is ideal — rainy season makes roads difficult
Your money goes extremely far here — $50/day is comfortable
flightFrom: Fly into Ngurah Rai International Airport (DPS)
cons: Traffic in south Bali is brutal
Limited ATM access in rural areas
travelCardTip: A no-foreign-transaction-fee card is essential. Citi Double Cash works great as a flat-rate card for all your spending. Carry some cash as many local warungs don't take cards.

name: Azores, Portugal
tagline: Europe's most underrated archipelago — whale watching, hot springs, and dramatic craters
bestFor: Nature lovers, hikers, whale watchers, and off-the-beaten-path travelers
highlights: Swim in Sete Cidades volcanic crater lake
Whale watching — one of the best spots in the world
Thermal hot springs at Furnas
Hiking Pico Mountain (highest peak in Portugal)
Surfing at Ribeira Grande (São Miguel)
tips: Island hop between São Miguel, Faial, Pico, and Flores
Rent a car on each island — public transport is very limited
Whale season is April–October for blue whales
Weather changes fast — pack layers even in summer
EU citizens fly here cheaply from Lisbon or Porto
flightFrom: Fly into Ponta Delgada (PDL) via Lisbon or direct from some US cities
pros: Affordable and off the tourist trail
travelCardTip: Chase Sapphire Preferred's trip delay insurance is valuable here — inter-island flights get cancelled frequently due to weather. No foreign transaction fee matters for Euro spending.

name: Croatia — Dalmatian Coast
tagline: Medieval walled cities, 1,000+ islands, and crystal-clear Adriatic water
bestFor: Sailors, history buffs, island hoppers, and Game of Thrones fans
highlights: Walk the walls of Dubrovnik's Old Town
Sail between Split, Hvar, Korčula, and Brač
Plitvice Lakes National Park
Hvar's lavender fields and nightlife
Kayak around Dubrovnik's city walls
tips: Dubrovnik is expensive in July/August — go in May or September
A sailing charter is the best way to see the islands
Hvar town is lively; Stari Grad on Hvar is quiet and beautiful
Croatia adopted the Euro in 2023 — no currency exchange needed from EU
Book Plitvice Lakes tickets online in advance
cons: Dubrovnik can feel like a theme park
travelCardTip: Now that Croatia uses the Euro, any no-foreign-transaction-fee card works perfectly. Amex Gold's dining multiplier is great for Croatia's incredible restaurant scene.

name: Turks & Caicos
tagline: The most pristine beaches in the Atlantic — Grace Bay is flawless
bestFor: Beach lovers, luxury travelers, and water sports enthusiasts
highlights: Grace Bay Beach — consistently ranked #1 in the world
Snorkel the world's third largest barrier reef
Kitesurfing at Long Bay
Whale watching (January–April)
Conch salad at the Conch Farm on Providenciales
tips: Providenciales (Provo) is the main island — that's where Grace Bay is
Rent a car to explore beyond Grace Bay Road
Visit the Salt Cay for an off-the-beaten-path Caribbean experience
USD is the local currency — no exchange needed
Hurricane season is June–November
flightFrom: Fly into Providenciales (PLS) — direct flights from many US cities
cons: Not as much cultural depth as other Caribbean islands
travelCardTip: Capital One Venture X's $300 travel credit and 10x on hotels booked through Capital One Travel makes a significant dent on luxury resort costs here.

name: Thailand Islands
tagline: Emerald waters, limestone cliffs, and legendary beach parties
bestFor: Budget backpackers, divers, party-goers, and island hoppers
highlights: Phi Phi Islands — Maya Bay and stunning cliffs
Full Moon Party on Koh Phangan
Scuba diving in the Similan Islands
Snorkeling at Koh Lanta
Rock climbing at Railay Beach, Krabi
tips: Koh Samui for comfort, Koh Tao for diving, Koh Phangan for parties
The Gulf of Thailand and Andaman coasts have different monsoon seasons
Ferries and speedboats connect islands cheaply
Never leave valuables on beaches
Street food is cheap and incredible — don't eat at tourist restaurants
travelCardTip: Carry cash — many Thai islands have limited card acceptance. Withdraw at ATMs using a card that reimburses foreign ATM fees. Schwab Bank debit card is popular for this.

name: Fiji
tagline: Bula! Soft coral capital of the world with warm Fijian hospitality
bestFor: Divers, honeymooners, families, and anyone seeking pure relaxation
highlights: Dive the Rainbow Reef — world's best soft coral
Remote Yasawa and Mamanuca Islands by catamaran
Traditional Fijian village visits and kava ceremonies
Surfing at Cloudbreak (one of the world's best waves)
Private island resorts
tips: The Mamanuca Islands are closest to Nadi — easiest to reach
Yasawa Flyer ferry is the classic backpacker route through the islands
Dry season (May–Oct) is best — wet season brings cyclone risk
Fijians are genuinely the friendliest people you'll meet
Budget for the seaplane or boat transfers — they add up
cons: Can be expensive on private islands
travelCardTip: Amex Platinum's travel protections and Global Entry credit are worth it for the long-haul flights required. Lounge access at LAX or SFO before a Pacific crossing makes a real difference.
